ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (rice noodles, cooked) is used as the closest parent. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for Lala White Bee Hoon as a composite seafood noodle dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One small serving of Lala White Bee Hoon is approximately 400 g, representing a shared portion for 1–3 people as served at Singaporean seafood restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Rice vermicelli with seafood, cooked — adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Lala White Bee Hoon exists in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by compositing: cooked rice vermicelli (HPB Foodo: bee hoon, cooked), lala clams (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: clams, cooked, moist heat, NDB 15159), and minor condiments (garlic, sesame oil, soy sauce, white pepper). Proportions estimated as approximately 55% bee hoon, 35% clams, 10% condiments/broth by cooked weight. Energy and macros are weighted composites. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: Rice vermicelli, cooked — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to Lala White Bee Hoon
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Lala White Bee Hoon. GI of 53 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked rice vermicelli (GI ~53, low category). The dish is predominantly rice vermicelli by weight (~55%), with clams and condiments contributing negligible glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as: (53 × 13.5 g carbohydrate per 100g × 400g serving) / 100 = 28.6 g. gi_category set to 'low' per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 320 mg per 100 g; a 400 g serving delivers approximately 1280 mg sodium
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a serving and request reduced soy sauce and seasoning, as a full small portion can contribute over 50% of the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load of approximately 28.6 g per 400 g serving despite a low GI of 53 for the rice vermicelli base
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a smaller portion (approximately 150–200 g) to keep glycemic load manageable, and pair the dish with additional non-starchy vegetables to slow glucose absorption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat content (~0.4 g per 100 g) and presence of omega-3 fatty acids from lala clams
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lala White Bee Hoon is relatively low in saturated fat and provides lean shellfish protein; individuals with cardiovascular disease may consume moderate portions while monitoring overall sodium intake from soy sauce and seasoning.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lala White Bee Hoon (Small) is a popular Singaporean Chinese seafood noodle dish featuring lala clams (Asian short-neck clams, Paphia undulata) cooked with thin rice vermicelli (bee hoon) in a light, milky-white broth. The dish is a signature offering at Singaporean seafood restaurants, most notably associated with establishments such as House of White Bee Hoon. The broth is typically prepared by simmering clams with garlic, ginger, spring onion, white pepper, and a small amount of sesame oil and light soy sauce, producing a delicate, slightly briny, and aromatic soup base. The rice vermicelli absorbs the broth and takes on a soft, silky texture, while the lala clams contribute a sweet, oceanic flavour and a tender bite. The small portion is marketed as suitable for 1–3 diners sharing. Nutritionally, the dish is moderate in energy (~98 kcal per 100 g), relatively high in protein from the clams (~7.2 g per 100 g), low in saturated fat (~0.4 g per 100 g), and moderate in sodium (~320 mg per 100 g) due to soy sauce and seasoning. The glycemic index of the rice vermicelli base is approximately 53 (low GI), though the glycemic load per full small serving (~400 g) is moderate at approximately 28.6 g. Clinically, sodium content is the primary concern for hypertension patients, while the moderate glycemic load warrants portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is not suitable for shellfish-allergic individuals and is not halal-certified in most restaurant contexts due to potential cross-contamination. It is dairy-free and gluten-reduced but not gluten-free due to soy sauce.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Lala White Bee Hoon exists in HPB Foodo or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing cooked rice vermicelli (HPB Foodo) and cooked clams (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy) in estimated proportions. GI is analogously estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked rice vermicelli. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates based on approximately 55% cooked rice vermicelli, 35% cooked lala clams, and 10% condiments/broth by cooked weight. Direct laboratory analysis of this dish would be required for high-confidence nutrient data. 2. Vitamin A (vitamin_a_mcg_rae) is -1 as it is not reliably reported in the component sources for this dish combination. 3. Vitamin B6 (vitamin_b6_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo bee hoon entry and the clam composite value is uncertain. 4. Folate (folate_mcg_dfe) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary HPB Foodo source for this dish type. 5. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this dish. 6. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this dish. 7. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source; clams are known to contain selenium but a defensible per-100g composite value cannot be confirmed without direct analysis. 8. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0 as this dish uses no hydrogenated fats; confirmed negligible by ingredient composition. 9. The halal status is marked false as lala clams are shellfish and the restaurant context (The Seafood Market by House of White Bee Hoon) does not carry a confirmed MUIS halal certification based on available public information. 10. Serving size of 400 g is an estimate for the small (S) portion based on typical Singaporean seafood restaurant portioning for a shared dish of this type. 11. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cooked rice vermicelli; no direct GI measurement for Lala White Bee Hoon has been published. 12. GL per serving = (53 × 13.5 / 100) × 400 = 28.6 g.
